canonical_score_source_required: archive.zip -> inflate.sh -> upstream/evaluate.py via experiments/contest_auth_eval.py --device cuda

dispatch_prerequisites: claim lane with tools/claim_lane_dispatch.py before any exact eval
build a deterministic archive with all score-affecting bytes charged
record archive bytes/SHA, runtime manifest, source archive custody, and build provenance
run experiments/contest_auth_eval.py --device cuda on archive.zip -> inflate.sh -> upstream/evaluate.py
treat the result as non-promotable until component gates and score recomputation pass
dispatch_recommendation.reason: best concrete candidate qzs3_b0512 locally beats the current renderer stream by 4354 bytes; exact CUDA is warranted only after building a deterministic archive and claiming the lane

imp_sparsity_prior.risk: Sparse bytes are not enough; trained IMP or sparse export still needs a charged decoder contract and exact CUDA component gates.
imp_sparsity_prior.use: sparsity prior only: rank tensor groups or future atoms; do not treat no-train pruning as scorer evidence

aggressive_prefixes.reason: frame2-only static head tensor group; lower direct PoseNet risk than shared trunk, frame1 FiLM head, or pose MLP

protected_prefixes.reason: shared representation feeds both frames; prior MQZ1 default-64 candidate showed PoseNet collapse
pose-conditioned frame head; keep at source QZS3 block size
direct pose-conditioning path; never made more aggressive in this policy

component_awareness.selection_rule: default to the exact-evaluated QZS3 b32 block size, protect all pose-critical prefixes, and shrink only frame2-only tensor groups

decision.reason: QBF1 remains negative evidence unless a concrete loader-ready layout beats both the decoded QZS3 renderer bytes and the current encoded renderer stream before exact CUDA is queued.

readiness.next_gate: Only implement a QBF1-v2 decoder/inflate path if a self-describing layout beats the current QZS3 renderer slice locally before exact CUDA auth eval is considered.
